title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 March 2025.

items.title: **NetEase** last-minute console delays console versions of hero shooter **FragPunk**
bullets: While the game will still be released for PC on March 6, the PS5 and Xbox Series S/X versions have been indefinitely delayed.
Announcing the delay on X, the FragPunk team said it had encountered “unexpected technical challenges in optimization and adaptation” during the console compliance testing process.

items.title: Obituary: Legacy of Kain writer **Jim Curry** has died
bullets: Along with Legacy of Kain: Soul Reaver, Curry wrote Akuji: The Heartless and handled marketing for the Gex games

items.title: **Sega** promises fewer new games but deeper investments in Sonic, Atlus, and Like a Dragon
bullets: SEGA plans to "reinforce" its core studios through hiring and mergers & acquisitions

items.title: 20-year-old browser game **Urban Dead** to shut down as result of UK's new online safety law
Translation studio **Transperfect** acquires support dev Technicolor Games
bullets: As part of Transperfect's game division, Technicolor will 'reinforce its reputation as a premier provider of art, animation, and VFX services to the global video game industry.'

items.title: Until Dawn remake studio **Ballistic Moon** "effectively closed"
bullets: Roughly 40 employees were let go in October, with remaining staff kept on to provide post-launch support for Ballistic's Until Dawn remake. However, it's claimed these developers - equating to around 20 employees - lost their jobs in December, leaving only the studio's founders and "possibly" a handful of employees "at most". Reportedly, Ballistic's entire PR, marketing, and development teams are now gone.
